title: Calling Aunt Sofia
summary: A girl calls her aunt in another country to wish her a happy birthday and tell her some small news.

body.speaker: Lia
body.text: Hello, Aunt Sofia! It is Lia.

body.speaker: Aunt Sofia
body.text: Oh, Lia! How lovely to hear you! How are you, my dear?

body.speaker: Lia
body.text: I am good, thank you. Happy birthday, Auntie!

body.speaker: Aunt Sofia
body.text: Thank you so much. You remembered.

body.speaker: Lia
body.text: Of course I did. What did you do today?

body.speaker: Aunt Sofia
body.text: I had breakfast with two friends. Then I went for a long walk by the river.

body.speaker: Aunt Sofia
body.text: Yes, the water was very still. Many small boats were on it.

body.speaker: Aunt Sofia
body.text: Not yet. I will have one tonight with your uncle. A lemon cake.

body.speaker: Lia
body.text: My favourite! Save me a small slice for next time.

body.speaker: Aunt Sofia
body.text: I will. Are you coming to visit this summer?

body.speaker: Lia
body.text: Yes! Mum says we will come in July. We will stay for two weeks.

body.speaker: Aunt Sofia
body.text: Two weeks! Wonderful. We can go to the beach.

body.speaker: Lia
body.text: Yes please! Okay, Auntie, Mum needs the phone now. Big hug!

body.speaker: Aunt Sofia
body.text: Big hug to you too, little Lia. Tell your mum hello.

annotations.meaningVi: Vui quá khi nghe được giọng cháu
annotations.exampleEn: How lovely to hear you on the phone!
My aunt makes a soft lemon cake.
Save me one slice of cake.
We visit our aunt every summer.
We will stay for two weeks.
We go to the beach in summer.

annotations.meaningVi: Ôm thật to (nói lúc tạm biệt)
annotations.exampleEn: Big hug to you too, Mum!

annotations.meaningVi: Cho cô gửi lời chào tới mẹ cháu nhé
annotations.exampleEn: Tell your mum hello from me.

grammarNotes.title: Simple past and past be
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi hỏi và trả lời về một ngày đã qua, ta dùng quá khứ đơn: 'What did you do today?' / 'I had breakfast with two friends.' / 'Then I went for a long walk.' / 'The water was very still.' Câu hỏi với 'did' giữ động từ ở dạng nguyên mẫu.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use simple past to ask and answer about a finished day: 'What did you do today?' / 'I had breakfast with two friends.' / 'Then I went for a long walk.' / 'The water was very still.' After 'did', use the base verb (no -ed).

grammarNotes.title: 'Will' for future plans
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Để nói kế hoạch tương lai, ta dùng 'will + động từ': 'I will have one tonight with your uncle.' / 'We will come in July.' / 'We will stay for two weeks.' Trong tiếng Anh nói, 'will' thường được viết tắt thành ''ll' (we'll, I'll).
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'will + verb' for future plans: 'I will have one tonight with your uncle.' / 'We will come in July.' / 'We will stay for two weeks.' In speech, 'will' is often shortened to ''ll' (we'll, I'll).

translationVi: Lia: A lô, cô Sofia! Cháu Lia đây ạ. Cô Sofia: Ôi Lia! Vui quá khi nghe được giọng cháu! Cháu thế nào rồi, cô bé yêu? Lia: Cháu khoẻ ạ. Chúc mừng sinh nhật cô! Cô Sofia: Cảm ơn cháu nhiều. Cháu vẫn nhớ. Lia: Đương nhiên rồi cô. Hôm nay cô làm gì ạ? Cô Sofia: Cô ăn sáng với hai người bạn. Rồi cô đi dạo dài bên bờ sông. Lia: Sông có đẹp không cô? Cô Sofia: Có chứ, mặt nước yên ả lắm. Có nhiều thuyền nhỏ trên sông. Lia: Cô ăn bánh chưa? Cô Sofia: Chưa. Tối nay cô sẽ ăn cùng dượng. Bánh chanh đó. Lia: Món yêu thích của cháu! Cô để dành cho cháu một miếng cho lần sau nhé. Cô Sofia: Cô sẽ để dành. Hè này cháu có sang chơi không? Lia: Có ạ! Mẹ nói tháng Bảy nhà cháu sang. Tụi cháu sẽ ở hai tuần. Cô Sofia: Hai tuần! Tuyệt quá. Mình có thể ra biển chơi. Lia: Vâng ạ! Thôi cô ơi, mẹ cần dùng điện thoại bây giờ. Ôm cô thật to! Cô Sofia: Cô cũng ôm cháu thật to nhé, Lia bé bỏng. Cho cô gửi lời chào tới mẹ cháu.

questions.prompt: Why is Lia calling her aunt?
options: To wish her a happy birthday

questions.prompt: What did Aunt Sofia do this morning?
Where did she go after breakfast?
options: For a long walk by the river
questions.explanation: 'I went for a long walk by the river.'

options: No, she does not like cake

questions.prompt: What kind of cake will she have tonight?
Who will she have it with?
What does Lia ask the aunt to do?
questions.explanation: 'Save me a small slice for next time.'

questions.prompt: Why does Lia end the call?
options: Her mum needs the phone now

Aunt Sofia: I had breakfast with two {{b3}}. Then I went for a long {{b4}} by the river.
Lia: Was the river beautiful?
Aunt Sofia: Yes, the water was very {{b5}}. Many small boats were on it.
Lia: Did you eat a cake?
Aunt Sofia: Not yet. I will have one tonight with your {{b6}}. A {{b7}} cake.
Lia: My favourite! Save me a small {{b8}} for next time.
Aunt Sofia: I will. Are you coming to visit this summer?
Lia: Yes! Mum says we will come in {{b9}}. We will stay for {{b10}} weeks.
Aunt Sofia: Two weeks! Wonderful. We can go to the {{b11}}.
Lia: Yes please! Okay, Auntie, Mum needs the phone now. Big {{b12}}!

criticalThinkingQuestion: Phone calls can keep families close even when they live in different countries. What is the best way to stay close with family far away?
